_readme: Reference/archive copy only — for actually importing into Label Studio Cloud, use the 3 separate files (elements/material_list/single) into their matching project. This file just groups all 3 together for browsing/backup.

data.file_warnings: This sheet (A-03) is the site-placement plan, distinct from the room-level floor plan (A-04, หน้า06) — same ground-floor building outline shown at 1:100, but this sheet's own content is the lot boundary, setback labels A/B, grading notes, and road, hence room-level detail is deliberately not repeated here. Only the ground floor is shown (site plans conventionally only show the footprint/roof outline, not the upper floor)., Notes wording (setback rule text, A/B definitions) matches houses #1-#3's equivalent A-03 sheet almost exactly (same government program template) aside from house-specific lot dimensions (13.70 x 16.20 here) and the A/0.60 side-setback zones flanking grid1/grid3., The 16.20m total lot depth reconciles cleanly against the internal 10.00m building-depth bracket (E-A), but the remaining 6.20m split between the top setback zone (above gridE) and bottom setback zone (below gridA, before the road) is not independently dimensioned on this sheet — flagged rather than guessed, similar in spirit to house #3's unresolved lot-depth gap (though here the gap is explained by genuinely undimensioned setback zones rather than a reconciliation mismatch)., GRID-RELEVANT (reporting only, master file not edited): the 8.50m building-width bracket (grid1-grid3, sub-split 5.00+3.50) and the 10.00m building-depth bracket (gridE-gridA, sub-split 2.70+2.30+3.50+1.50) printed on this site plan exactly match the หน้า00_gridline.json master grid (x: 1=0,2=5.0,3=8.5; y: E=0,D=2.7,C=5.0,B=8.5,A=10.0) — fully cross-consistent. This sheet does NOT show the master's dummy grid E' (-1.9m, from the structural S-02/S-03 footing plans) since this site plan's building footprint starts exactly at gridE with no extension beyond it visible at this scale/resolution — not independent confirmation either way, just not visible here.

data.file_warnings: This is the ground-floor plan sheet (A-04, แปลนพื้นชั้น 1) of a 2-story house — the roof plan is on its own separate sheet (A-06, หน้า08), and the 2nd floor is on its own separate sheet (A-05, หน้า07, see sibling _หน้า07_floor_plan_upper.json)., This house has THREE distinct stair-related callouts printed on the ground floor (ST1/A-19 at the เฉลียง threshold step, and a dual ST2/A-20 + ST3/A-21 callout both pointing at the single main staircase icon in โถง) plus a possible 4th ambiguous callout near the east wall past เฉลียง — unlike house #3 which had only 2 stairs (ST.1 porch step, ST.2 main switchback). This house's A-01 index also printed 'แบบขยายบันได ST.3' for BOTH A-21 and A-22 (see _หน้า03_index.json), consistent with ST.3 being a 2-sheet detail (plan A-21 + section/hardware A-22, mirroring house #3's ST.2 pattern) — but which physical stair is 'ST2' vs 'ST3' on this floor plan is not conclusively resolved here; flagged for cross-check once A-19/A-20/A-21/A-22 (หน้า19-22) are read., GRID-RELEVANT FOR หน้า00 MASTER (reporting only, master file not edited): top dimension chain reads left to right: 0.60 (west overhang, beyond grid1) | 5.00 (grid1-2, sub-split 2.30+2.70, further sub-split 1.10+1.20 within the 2.30 zone) | 3.50 (grid2-3) | 0.60 (east overhang, beyond grid3) — matches the master grid's x_lines (1=0.0, 2=5.0, 3=8.5) exactly, no dummy grid needed on the x-axis for this sheet. Left dimension chain reads top to bottom: 2.00 (north overhang zone, above gridE — this genuinely resolves part of the site plan's (หน้า05) previously-flagged undimensioned top setback zone) | 2.70 (gridE-D) | 2.30 (gridD-C) | 10.00 bracket total (E to A) | 3.50 (gridC-B) | 1.50 (gridB-A) | 1.60 (south, below gridA, before the hatched grading wedge) — matches the master grid's y_lines (E=0.0, D=2.7, C=5.0, B=8.5, A=10.0) exactly. Combined with the site plan's total lot depth of 16.20m, this floor plan's own drawn extent (2.00+10.00+1.60=13.60m) accounts for most but not all of the lot depth — the remaining ~2.60m is presumably further front/back yard beyond what this floor-plan sheet dimensions, consistent with the site plan showing extra road-frontage setback not repeated here., The master grid's dummy row E' (-1.9m, from structural S-02/S-03 pile/footing plans) is NOT visible as a distinct dimensioned zone on this architecture floor plan — the 2.00m zone above gridE (ซักล้าง/ดินถม area) is a different, smaller offset than the structural E' dummy grid's 1.9m; not treated as confirming or contradicting the structural dummy grid, just noting they are not the same feature., Individual door/window counts and the circle-vs-box (door-vs-window) glyph distinction per room, plus several small triangle vent/fixture symbols, were not confidently resolvable at this image resolution — flagged for zoom review rather than guessed., Elevation-direction callout bubble (bottom of sheet: 3/A-09 top, 2/A-08 left, 4/A-10 right, 1/A-07 bottom) and section-cut callout arrows (tags '1/A-11' appearing twice, '2/A-12' appearing twice, '3/A-13' appearing twice, at various points around the plan perimeter) are printed on this sheet — read and cross-referenced when extracting those sheets, not re-itemized as elements here., WC1 is detailed on its own dedicated 1:25 sheet (A-16, หน้า16) — this floor-plan-level box is superseded by that sheet for exact fixture layout; see _หน้า16_wc1_plan.json (to be written when that page is read). Unlike house #3 (WC.1+WC.2 sharing one detail sheet), this house has 3 separate WC detail sheets (A-16/A-17/A-18 for WC.1/WC.2/WC.3) per the index — only WC1 appears on this ground floor plan.

data.file_warnings: This is the 2nd-floor plan sheet (A-05, แปลนพื้นชั้น 2) — ground floor is _หน้า06_floor_plan_ground.json, roof plan is _หน้า08_roof_plan.json (not yet read)., This house has 3 bedrooms (นอน1/นอน2/นอน3) plus a dedicated แต่งตัว (dressing room) and 2 upstairs bathrooms (ห้องน้ำ2/ห้องน้ำ3, WC.2/WC.3) all on this 2nd floor — WC.1 (ground floor) + WC.2 + WC.3 (this floor) account for all 3 bathroom detail sheets (A-16/A-17/A-18) referenced in the index., GRID-RELEVANT FOR หน้า00 MASTER (reporting only, master file not edited): top dimension chain matches the master grid's x_lines (1=0.0, 2=5.0, 3=8.5) exactly — 0.60 (west overhang) | 5.00 (grid1-2, sub-split 3.30+1.70 for นอน3/ห้องน้ำ3) | 3.50 (grid2-3) | 0.60 (east overhang). Left dimension chain matches the master grid's y_lines (E=0.0, D=2.7, C=5.0, B=8.5, A=10.0) exactly — 1.10 (north overhang, above gridE, smaller than the ground floor's 2.00m overhang at the same edge since the ground floor's ซักล้าง room extends further north than the 2nd floor's roof overhang) | 2.70 (E-D) | 2.30 (D-C) | 10.00 bracket (E-A) | 3.50 (C-B, sub-split 1.60+1.90) | 1.50 (B-A)., Elevation-direction callout bubble (3/A-09 top, 2/A-08 left, 4/A-10 right, 1/A-07 bottom) and section-cut callout arrows ('1/A-11', '2/A-12', '3/A-13' at multiple points) match the ground floor plan's equivalent callouts exactly — cross-consistent., The step-numbering continuity finding (steps 5-17 on this floor continuing from 1-6 on the ground floor, all under a single 'ST3/A-21' callout on this sheet) is reported here as evidence toward resolving the ground floor's flagged ST2-vs-ST3 dual-callout ambiguity, but is not treated as fully conclusive until the actual stair detail sheets (A-19/A-20/A-21/A-22, หน้า19-22) are read later in this same extraction pass.

data.file_warnings: This sheet (A-06) is the roof plan for the whole 2-story building — since the roof sits above the 2nd floor (+6.65 roof beam level, inferred from the same value as house #3's canopy level), its footprint corresponds to the 2nd-floor plan (หน้า07/A-05), not the ground floor., Unlike house #3's roof plan (which had a rectangular notch over an open, roofless carport), this house's roof is a single unbroken rectangle over the full grid1-3 x gridE-A footprint plus overhangs — suggesting the ground-floor จอดรถ (carport, see _หน้า06_floor_plan_ground.json) is fully roofed here, not open to the sky. Not independently confirmed against a structural roof-framing sheet (outside this extraction's architecture-only scope)., The 'SLOPE 6°' labels are printed vertically along both roof faces, same convention and same 6-degree value as houses #2/#3's roof plans., Overhang dimensions (1.30m east/west, 1.50m north/south) exactly match houses #2/#3's equivalent overhang figures despite this house having a different footprint size (8.50x10.00m vs house #3's), and are independently corroborated by the top/bottom dimension chains on the floor plan sheets (หน้า06/07) which show the same building-width/depth sub-splits., Actual roof framing (purlins/rafters/trusses) is not detailed on this sheet — references to structural engineering drawings would be expected (outside this extraction's architecture-only page range 03-29), consistent with the section sheets to be read later (หน้า13-15)., Two dashed rectangular outlines near grid2 (north and south edges) and two curved swirl-arrow symbols near mid-plan are visible but not confidently classified at this resolution — flagged rather than guessed; may become clearer when cross-checked against the elevation/section sheets.

data.file_warnings: B4/B4A/B4X/B5 all carry a '(หยุดที่ L/8)' curtailment note on their bottom bars -- same unusual pattern flagged in house #3's B4, now seen recurring in this house too across 4 marks. This is looking like a genuine, deliberate design detail in this design-office's standard rather than a one-off misread -- still worth a human structural review to confirm the intended meaning, but no longer treating it as likely OCR error., B4A/B4X are deep beams (700mm) with variable stirrup spacing (tighter 0.20m near supports, wider 0.50m mid-span) plus extra 2-Ø9mm skin bars -- the flat width_mm/height_mm/main_bar/stirrup schema doesn't fully capture this nuance; recorded with a single representative stirrup spacing (0.20m, the more critical support-zone value) and the variability noted in the stirrup.note field.

data.file_warnings: This sheet shows two nearly-symmetric riser systems side by side (left stack + right stack) both feeding into the same Ø4"SW discharge run to the septic tank, and a shared water-supply/booster-pump/tank system on the right side of the sheet -- likely representing two separate bathroom stacks (e.g. ground-floor WC and upper-floor WC vertically aligned) rather than one system, consistent with this being a 2-story house. Not cross-confirmed against the floor plans yet (pages 53-54) -- flagged for review once those are read., LEVEL LABEL: same pattern noted on house #3's equivalent sheet -- 'ระดับพื้นชั้นล่าง' (ground-floor level) is printed at BOTH +3.75 and +0.60, which is physically implausible if both are literally 'ground floor'. Unlike house #3's sheet (which mislabeled its lowest level as 'ระดับพื้นชั้นหลังคา'), this page's lowest level is labeled 'ระดับดิน' (ground/soil level) which is internally consistent. Transcribed literally as printed; the '+3.75 ระดับพื้นชั้นล่าง' label likely should read something like 'ระดับพื้นชั้นบน' (upper floor) based on the same reasoning applied to house #3 (a floor at +3.75 is far too high to be ground floor) -- to be cross-confirmed once the upper-floor sanitary plan (expected around page54) is read., The two ⊣⊢-style glyphs near the +3.75 level on each stack's vertical pipe are not separately labeled with text on the sheet -- described by position/appearance only, flagged for zoom-review, same treatment as the analogous unlabeled valve pair on house #3's equivalent page., Booster pump brand list reads 'FUJKCA' on this sheet's text block (vs. 'FUJIKA' transcribed on house #3's equivalent sheet) -- transcribed exactly as printed here, not harmonized; could be an OCR/print-quality artifact of the same brand name rather than a genuine difference, not independently verifiable at this resolution., Water tank, booster pump, and septic tank equipment-spec text blocks are clearly legible, transcribed in full with high confidence. This equipment_specs boilerplate matches the same text seen on house #3's page49 riser diagram and page50 floor plan., grid_ref_is_free_text_not_grid_notation is correctly true here -- this is a schematic vertical diagram, not on the building's structural plan grid.

data.file_warnings: POSSIBLE DUPLICATE SEPTIC TANK LABEL: this plan appears to show the text/leader 'ถังบำบัดน้ำเสียสำเร็จรูป' pointing to TWO separate circle symbols -- one small circle near the top (just below the booster pump, on the tank/pump-side Ø4"SW line) and one large circle at bottom-left (receiving the ห้องน้ำ 1 stack's Ø4"SW line). This could mean the house has 2 separate septic-tank units (plausible for a 2-story house with fixtures on both the utility/kitchen side and the bathroom side), OR the top instance could be a mislabeled/misread grease-trap-adjacent symbol. Not resolved at this resolution -- flagged as 'septic_tank_or_pretreatment_unit' with reduced confidence for the smaller top instance pending human zoom-review; the bottom-left large tank is transcribed with normal confidence as the main septic_tank., Room level labels differ from house #3's equivalent ground-floor plan at several positions (ซักล้าง +0.50 here vs +0.20 there; เก็บของ +0.30 here vs +0.60 there; ห้องน้ำ 1 +0.20 here vs +0.50 there) -- transcribed exactly as printed on this house's own drawing, not harmonized to house #3's values; expected to differ since this is a different house design., Grid (1,2,3 x E,D,C,B,A) confirmed identical to the structural grid master (หน้า00_gridline.json) built from this house's S-02/S-03 sheets -- no dummy grid line (E') is drawn on this sanitary/architecture sheet, consistent with the master's note that the dummy row is a structural-footing-only finding., Exact routing of individual small-diameter branch CW/W pipes into each room, and the precise count/position of every perimeter X-in-square catch-basin symbol (~8 counted, approximate), is difficult to trace precisely at this resolution -- flagged for zoom-review, consistent with how house #3's equivalent floor-plan page was handled., Booster pump brand list here reads 'MITSUBISHI, HITACHI, FUJIKA' (matches house #3's spelling) -- note this differs from this same house's own riser-diagram sheet (page52/SN-04), which read 'FUJKCA' at the same position; both transcribed exactly as printed on their respective pages, flagged as an internal print/OCR inconsistency worth a human check rather than corrected to match one or the other., This ground-floor plan is expected to pair with an upper-floor plan on the next sheet (SN-06, page54) -- see that file's own extraction for any cross-check on the +3.75/+0.60 level-label ambiguity flagged on this house's riser diagram (page52).

data.file_warnings: LEVEL LABEL CROSS-CHECK CONFIRMED: every room on this upper-floor plan is at +3.75 (or +3.65 for wet areas/balcony) -- this directly confirms that the riser diagram on page52 (SN-04), which printed 'ระดับพื้นชั้นล่าง +3.75' (labeling +3.75 as if it were the ground floor), is very likely a mislabeled/boilerplate-reused level tag on that sheet: +3.75 is actually the upper floor ('ชั้นบน'), matching this house's own SN-06 plan. This is the same finding pattern already documented for house #3's equivalent riser diagram (page49) via its own upper-floor plan (page51). Flagged for human review; not corrected on the riser diagram file itself per extraction rules (transcribe literally, flag discrepancy)., Stair-step numbering is a continuation from the ground-floor plan's steps (which ended at step 6, labeled 'ขึ้น'): this sheet shows steps continuing as roughly 7 through 17 with a mid-landing around 12, labeled 'ขึ้น' again at the top of this flight -- consistent with a switchback (dog-leg) staircase connecting the two floors. Exact per-step numbering hard to fully verify pixel-by-pixel at this resolution; overall sequence transcribed as a range (6/7-11, landing 12, 13-17), not a guaranteed exact digit-by-digit read., The two small bathrooms (ห้องน้ำ 2, ห้องน้ำ 3) contain fixture icon clusters that are visually present but not individually identifiable with confidence (WC vs LAV vs floor drain) at this image resolution -- flagged for zoom-review rather than guessed., The small square symbol on the roofline above ห้องน้ำ 3 is ambiguous (possible AVC vent-stack roof penetration, consistent with the vent pipes seen running to roof level on the riser diagram) but not labeled with text at this position -- flagged for zoom-review., Grid confirmed identical to the ground-floor plan (page53) and the structural grid master; only the top overhang dimension differs (1.10 vs 2.00), which is an architectural roofline value, not a grid discrepancy.

data.file_warnings: This is the second of 2 mechanical sheets in the drawing set (M-02 of 2) -- together with M-01 (page 65) it covers this house's full ventilation-fan/AC layout, split by floor., GRID: dimension chain matches the upper-floor electrical sheets and the master grid file exactly., This floor carries all 3 of this house's wall-type AC units (9,000 + 15,000 + 18,000 BTU) and 5 ceiling-fan/exhaust-grille pairs (40+120+40+120+60 CFM) -- the ground floor (M-01, page 65) has none of either, since all 3 bedrooms are upstairs, same pattern as house #3., GOOD NEWS (contrast with house #3): the largest AC unit here is 18,000 BTU, which IS fully covered by EE-06's AC circuit-sizing table (page 64, covers up to 18,000 BTU/hr) -- unlike house #3, which had a 24,000 BTU unit exceeding its equivalent table's coverage. No spec gap found on this house., Several exhaust-grille/ceiling-fan pairs are positioned close together near room boundaries (ensuite bathroom vs นอน 3, and the นอน 2/โถง cluster) -- room attribution for those is a best-effort proximity read based on which wet-area icon or room boundary each callout leader line points nearest to, not confirmed against an explicit per-room label; flagged 'room_attribution_by_proximity'., One exhaust grille callout near แต่งตัว is printed as 'EXHAUST AIR GRILL 6"x6"' without the 'W/INS' (with insulation) suffix used on every other grille on this sheet and on M-01 -- transcribed exactly as printed rather than assumed to be an omission., Small stair-tread numbers printed near the staircase (two stacked rows of digits) are structural/architectural (tread count for the two flights), not a mechanical element -- excluded from 'elements'., A dashed 'แนวหลังคา' (roof line) at the bottom of the plan is architectural, not mechanical -- excluded, consistent with the same annotation excluded on EE-04 (page 62).

data.file_warnings: All figures transcribed from Thai numerals (๐-๙) on a grayscale/low-contrast printed table -- individual digit misreads are possible despite reasonable confidence; recommend spot-checking against the source if these figures feed into any downstream cost model., House #6's row shows ฐานรากแผ่ (spread footing, 1,227,000 baht) MORE EXPENSIVE than ฐานรากเสาเข็ม (pile foundation, 1,102,000 baht) -- the reverse of every other row on this table (pile foundation is consistently pricier elsewhere, as expected since piling adds cost). This exact same anomaly pattern (one house's pricing reversed) was also flagged in house #3's equivalent reference table for a different house number -- worth a consolidated human review across all houses' pricing tables rather than treating each as an isolated one-off., House #10's pile-foundation cost (2,945,000) is disproportionately higher than its spread-footing cost (2,035,000) -- a ~45% gap vs the ~5-10% gap seen in every other row -- flagged as a likely transcription-worthy anomaly (possibly a genuine outlier for a larger/heavier design, or a digit misread of 2,145,000 or similar; not resolved here).

{
  "source_image": "image/บ้าน_เล็ก_2ชั้น_02/บ้าน_เล็ก_2ชั้น_02_หน้า50.png",
  "schema_generation": "primary_rawjson_schema.md (2026-07-10 compiled)",
  "view_title": "รายการประกอบแบบการเดินท่อภายในอาคาร ( แผ่นที่ 1 )",
  "drawing_no": "SN-58081",
  "notes_sections": [
    {
      "heading": "1. ข้อกำหนดทั่วไป",
      "items": [
        "1.1 ท่อและอุปกรณ์ต่างๆ ที่นำมาใช้ ต้องเป็นของใหม่อยู่ในสภาพสามารถใช้งานได้ดี ไม่เคยนำไปใช้ที่อื่นมาก่อน และจะต้องดำเนินการติดตั้งตามคำแนะนำของบริษัทผู้ผลิตอย่างถูกต้อง",
        "1.2 แบบและรายการที่แสดงไว้ เป็นเพียงแนวทางเท่านั้น ในทางปฏิบัติสามารถเสนอวิธีการอื่นที่ดีกว่า โดยไม่ขัดกับแนวทางที่กำหนดไว้ได้ แต่ต้องได้รับความเห็นชอบเป็นลายลักษณ์อักษรจากผู้ว่าจ้างเสียก่อน",
        "1.3 สุขภัณฑ์ทุกจุดจะต้องมีการเดินท่อระบบสุขาภิบาลไปถึงในกรณีที่แบบแปลนมิได้แสดงรายละเอียดไว้ ให้ใช้ขนาดท่อและวิธีการปฏิบัติเช่นเดียวกับสุขภัณฑ์ชนิดเดียวกันกับจุดอื่นๆ",
        "1.4 อุปกรณ์อื่นเช่น ประตูน้ำ ข้อต่อ ข้องอ ประตูน้ำกันน้ำย้อนกลับ หรืออื่นๆ ที่จำเป็นต้องใช้ในการติดตั้ง เพื่อให้งานดีขึ้นและถูกต้องตามหลักวิชาการ แม้มิได้ระบุไว้ในแบบ ผู้รับจ้างต้องจัดหาและทำการติดตั้งให้โดยไม่คิดราคาเพิ่ม",
        "1.5 หลังจากเดินท่อสุขาภิบาลในช่องท่อแล้ว ที่ระดับพื้นแต่ละชั้นให้เทคอนกรีตเสริมเหล็กปิดช่องท่อให้เรียบร้อย",
        "1.6 หากมีการประกาศกำหนดเป็นมาตรฐานผลิตภัณฑ์อุตสาหกรรม (มอก.) แล้ว ให้ใช้ผลิตภัณฑ์ที่ได้มาตรฐานผลิตภัณฑ์อุตสาหกรรม (มอก.) นั้น ถ้ายังไม่มีผู้ผลิตได้รับมาตรฐาน ให้ใช้ผลิตภัณฑ์ผลิตได้เทียบเท่าแต่ยังไม่ได้รับตรามาตรฐานก็ได้",
        "1.7 การจัดทำแบบ",
        "1.7.1 ผู้รับจ้างต้องจัดทำแบบ SHOP DRAWING เสนอผู้ควบคุมงานของผู้ว่าจ้างอนุมัติก่อนดำเนินการ ซึ่งต้องยึดถือหลักวิชาการและต้องไม่เป็นการตัดทอนหรือลดเนื้องานไปจากแบบ ทั้งนี้งานเพิ่มจาก SHOP DRAWING นี้จะขอคิดเงินหรือขยายเวลาเพิ่มไม่ได้ ยกเว้นได้รับอนุมัติจากผู้ว่าจ้าง",
        "1.7.2 ก่อนส่งงานงวดสุดท้าย ผู้รับจ้างจะต้องส่งแบบ ASBUILT DRAWING ให้ผู้ว่าจ้าง 5 ชุด",
        "1.8 วัสดุและอุปกรณ์ที่ต้องได้รับการอนุมัติก่อนดำเนินการติดตั้ง มีดังต่อไปนี้",
        "1.8.1 ท่อและข้อต่อ",
        "1.8.2 ช่องระบายน้ำทิ้งที่พื้น, ช่องทำความสะอาดที่พื้น, ตะแกรงกันผงปิดช่องระบายน้ำฝน",
        "1.8.3 เครื่องสูบน้ำ",
        "1.8.4 อุปกรณ์ระบบบำบัดน้ำเสีย",
        "1.8.5 อุปกรณ์ระบบดับเพลิง",
        "1.8.6 ประตูน้ำต่างๆ",
        "1.9 การเชื่อมต่อท่อประปาและท่อระบายน้ำจากภายนอกเข้ามายังอาคาร",
        "1.9.1 ผู้รับจ้างจะต้องจัดหาและดำเนินการต่อท่อน้ำประปาจากภายนอกเข้ามายังโครงการ ถ้ามิได้ระบุไว้เป็นอย่างอื่น ค่าใช้จ่ายในการจัดหาท่อน้ำประปาและติดตั้งมาตรวัดน้ำ เป็นของผู้รับจ้างทั้งสิ้น (ทั้งนี้ไม่รวมถึงการขยายเขตการใช้น้ำประปา)",
        "1.9.2 ผู้รับจ้างจะต้องดำเนินการต่อท่อระบายน้ำจากอาคารลงสู่ทางระบายน้ำภายนอก หรือทางระบายน้ำสาธารณะ ค่าใช้จ่ายในการขออนุญาต (ถ้ามี) และการต่อเชื่อมประสานท่อ เป็นของผู้รับจ้างทั้งสิ้น",
        "1.10 การรับประกัน ผู้รับจ้างต้องรับประกันคุณภาพและสมรรถนะของเครื่องจักรและอุปกรณ์ภายในระยะเวลา 2 ปี นับจากวันที่ส่งมอบงานงวดสุดท้าย"
      ]
    },
    {
      "heading": "2. ท่อและอุปกรณ์ท่อ ประตูน้ำและอุปกรณ์ประกอบ การเลือกใช้ท่อประเภทใด ให้ปฏิบัติตามรายการ ประตูน้ำชนิดต่างๆ ต้องใช้ชนิดทนความดันใช้งานไม่น้อยกว่า Class 125",
      "items": [
